title: Path Sum II
problem_statement: Find all root-to-leaf paths in a binary tree where the sum of node values equals targetSum.
constraints: The number of nodes is in the range [0, 5000]
intuition: Explore each root-to-leaf path with DFS, tracking the current sum and backtracking when paths no longer match the target.

approaches.name: DFS backtracking
approaches.description: Traverse recursively, track the current path and remaining sum, and add valid root-to-leaf paths to the result.
approaches.time: O(n^2) in worst case due to path copies
approaches.why_not: BFS is harder for root-to-leaf path collection and requires additional tracking for partial paths.
interview_commentary: Backtracking adds and removes nodes as the path changes.
Only add paths at leaf nodes when the remaining sum matches.
Copy the path before adding to results to preserve state.

interview_qa.q: How do you maintain the current path?
interview_qa.a: Use a list that adds the current node before recursion and removes it after exploring children.

interview_qa.q: When do you record a valid path?
interview_qa.a: When you reach a leaf and the remaining sum equals the leaf’s value.

interview_qa.q: Why copy the path when adding to results?
interview_qa.a: Because the path list is modified during backtracking.
O(n^2) when many paths exist and each path is copied into the result.
